findings.finding: When the pane's args already contain a --resume/--continue token, the respawn reuses that pre-existing (possibly stale) conversation pointer instead of the live captured session id (sid). For a pane launched with `--resume <oldId>`, the switched-in pane would resume oldId rather than the conversation's current live id. Uncommon config (panes are normally launched without --resume), and the guard is deliberate to avoid duplicating the flag, so this is a minor correctness edge, not a strand/loss.
findings.fix: Optional: if an existing --resume carries a different id than sid, prefer sid (rewrite the token) rather than skipping; or document that switchProfile assumes the pane was not manually pinned to another --resume id.

findings.finding: After a successful switch the old pane is removed from `panes`, but its key lingers forever in the autoContinued/switchAttempted Sets, contJitterMs, and (if the new pane never emits an id) pendingContinue. Since the re-arm delete at :307-309 only runs for panes still present, these entries are never cleaned. This is a tiny, bounded memory retention, not a correctness bug (the vanished key is never iterated again since :303 loops live panes only), so no ping-pong or double action results.
findings.evidence: panes = panes.filter((x) => x.key !== p.key);  // old key stays in autoContinued/switchAttempted
findings.fix: Optional: on successful switch, also autoContinued.delete(p.key)/switchAttempted.delete(p.key)/delete contJitterMs[p.key] for the removed key to keep the Sets from accumulating over long sessions.
